failure_surface: Formulas written to cells E2:E6 contain double equals signs (==MEDIAN...) instead of single equals (=MEDIAN...), causing them to be stored as text strings rather than executable Excel formulas.
root_cause: The agent constructed formula strings that already included the leading '=' character, then prefixed them again with '=' when assigning to cell values via openpyxl (using f'={formula}'), resulting in invalid '==...' syntax that Excel treats as plain text.
minimal_fix_hypothesis: When assigning formula strings to cells via openpyxl, check whether the formula string already includes the '=' prefix before deciding whether to add one - do not double-prefix formulas.
evidence: Verification output shows: ('==MEDIAN(IF(($C$2:$C$12>=0)*($C$2:$C$12<=0.01),IF(ROW($A$2:$A$12)>=2,$B$2:$B$12)))',) for cell E2
All five cells (E2:E6) exhibit the same double-equals pattern
Code section: formulas dict defines values like '=MEDIAN(...)' with leading '=', then assigns using f'={formula}'
openpyxl cell.value assignment does not strip the extra '=' - it stores exactly what's assigned

memory_items.title: Formula Prefix Consistency
memory_items.lesson: When writing Excel formulas via openpyxl, ensure the formula string has exactly one leading '=' - check if your string builder already includes it before adding another.
memory_items.generalization: Formula strings in Python-to-Excel workflows must be validated for correct syntax before assignment; double-prefixing converts formulas to static text.

memory_items.title: Formula Assignment Pattern
memory_items.lesson: Either store formulas without '=' and add it during assignment, or store with '=' and assign directly - never both.
memory_items.generalization: Establish a consistent convention for formula string construction across all spreadsheet manipulation tasks to avoid syntax corruption.

memory_items.title: Post-Write Formula Verification
memory_items.lesson: After writing formulas via openpyxl, verify they are recognized as formulas (not text) by checking cell.value doesn't show duplicated prefix characters.
memory_items.generalization: Always spot-check critical formula cells after programmatic writing to catch syntax errors before saving the workbook.

edits.content:
## Formula Syntax Hygiene
- **Single Equals Rule:** Formulas written via openpyxl must contain exactly one leading '=' character. Do not double-prefix (e.g., f'={formula}' where formula already starts with '=')
- **Assignment Convention:** Establish a consistent pattern: either omit '=' in formula strings and add during assignment, OR include '=' in strings and assign directly.
- **Verification Step:** After writing formulas programmatically, verify cell values start with single '=' to confirm they will execute as formulas, not display as text.
